name: Satoru Gojo.
description:
Snap out of it---♡︎¿ Why can you love me like you love them?

Creator’s Note:

HELLOOO AGAIN. Yeah, I know. Two bots back-to-back? Me?

Before anything else:

Yes, I tried to embed Snap Out of It here with a cute little iframe like a real tech-savvy bot dev.

No, it didn’t work.

Apparently, I can write heartbreaking Gojo scenarios but I cannot defeat HTML.

I will simply cry and leave you with emotional damage instead.

Now, let’s address the glowing, blindfolded elephant in the room—

Where have I been?

The short version:

I took a break. A long one. Like... two months. Not because I stopped loving Gojo (never that), but because life decided to swing a baseball bat straight at my motivation. Between real-life chaos, burnout, and just existing like a sleep-deprived NPC, I had to step back. It wasn’t planned. I thought I’d be gone for a week max. Then suddenly I blinked and it was May. Love that.

BUT I’m back now (kinda?) and I missed this. I missed you all. I missed the comments. The chaos. The crying. The way y’all project onto these pixel men and turn scenarios into therapy sessions—I LIVE FOR IT.

This bot, inspired by Snap Out of It by Arctic Monkeys, is a little different. It’s sharp. A little bitter. Messy. Because Gojo doesn’t always cry pretty. Sometimes he gets jealous. Sometimes he says the wrong thing. Sometimes he walks away even when he wants to stay. And I wanted to capture that side of him—the human one.

So yeah. Two bots. One emotional breakdown. A failed iframe.

P.S. Stream Arctic Monkeys or Gojo will show up in your kitchen at 3AM, blindfold on, blasting British indie rock and asking why you didn’t love him enough.
